parameters.name: file
parameters.label: File
parameters.description: The path and name of the image or movie file to load. Image and movie formats are those found in .  You can specify files on the internet using ...

parameters.name: reload
parameters.label: Reload
parameters.description: Change from 0 to 1 to force the image to reload, useful when the file changes or did not exist at first.

parameters.name: reloadpulse
parameters.label: Reload Pulse
parameters.description: Instantly reloads the file.

parameters.name: playmode
parameters.label: Play Mode
parameters.description: Specifies the method used to play the movie, there are 3 options.

parameters.name: play
parameters.label: Play
parameters.description: Movie plays when 1, movie stops when 0.

parameters.name: speed
parameters.label: Speed
parameters.description: This is a speed multiplier which only works when Play Mode is

parameters.name: cue
parameters.label: Cue
parameters.description: Jumps to Cue Point when set to 1. Only available when Play Mode is Sequential.

parameters.name: cuepulse
parameters.label: Cue Pulse
parameters.description: Instantly jumps to the Cue Point position in the movie.

parameters.name: cuepoint
parameters.label: Cue Point
parameters.description: Set any index in the movie as a point to jump to.

parameters.name: cuepointunit
parameters.label: Cue Point Unit
parameters.description: Select the units for this parameter from Index, Frames, Seconds, and Fraction (percentage).

parameters.name: cuebehavior
parameters.label: Cue Behavior
parameters.description: Customize the Cue parameter's behavior.

parameters.name: index
parameters.label: Index
parameters.description: This parameter explicitly sets the movie position when Play Mode is set to Specify Index. The units menu on the right lets you specify the index in the following units: Index, Frames, Seconds, and Fra

parameters.name: indexunit
parameters.label: Index Unit
parameters.description: Select the units for this parameter from Index, Frames, Seconds, and Fraction (percentage).

parameters.name: loopcrossfade
parameters.label: Loop Crossfade
parameters.description: Crossfades the beginning and end of the movie together to create a smooth transition when looping. If the movie uses Trim options, it will crossfade Trim Start with Trim End positions.

parameters.name: loopcrossfadeunit
parameters.label: Loop Crossfade Unit
parameters.description: Select the units for this parameter from Index, Frames, Seconds, and Fraction (percentage).

parameters.name: stepsize
parameters.label: Step Size
parameters.description: Sets how many frames to skip before displaying next frame. For example, a StepSize of 30 will display every 30th frame. This timing of movie playback does not change, so with a Step Size of 30 and a s

parameters.name: audioloop
parameters.label: Audio Loop
parameters.description: This menu helps you determine how to treat the audio as the end of a movie approaches. This is needed because of all the cases of playing a movie, like when driving with an index, the will not know if

parameters.name: imageindexing
parameters.label: Image Sequence Indexing
parameters.description: Determines how an image sequence is ordered.

parameters.name: timecodeop
parameters.label: Timecode Object/CHOP/DAT
parameters.description: Set the movie index with a reference to a timecode. Should be a reference to either a with channels 'hour', 'second', 'minute', 'frame', a with a timecode string in its first cell, or a object.

parameters.name: interp
parameters.label: Interpolate Frames
parameters.description: Interpolates between frames based based on exact time. For example, if the index (in frames) is 1.5, then frames 1 and 2 will be blended 50-50. If the index is 1.7 then 30% of frame 1 is blended with

parameters.name: deinterlace
parameters.label: Deinterlace
parameters.description: For movies that are stored as fields, where each image is made of two images interleaved together. A 30-frame per second movie would contain 60 fields per second. For each image, the even scanlines of

parameters.name: precedence
parameters.label: Field Precedence
parameters.description: Where fields are extracted one field at a time, this will extract the Even field first by default, otehrwise it will extract the odd field first. The video industry has not standardized on one or the

parameters.name: bottomhalfalpha
parameters.label: Bottom Half is Alpha (AAA)
parameters.description: If enabled, the image/movie will have it's height halved, and the R channel from the pixels on the bottom half will be treated as if they were the alpha channel for the top half. Useful for cases wher

parameters.name: premultrgbbyalpha
parameters.label: Pre-Multiply RGB by Alpha
parameters.description: Premultiplies the image.

parameters.name: inputcolorspace
parameters.label: Input Color Space
parameters.description: Controls what color space the input data will be treated as. It will be converted to the Working when uploaded to the .

parameters.name: inputreferencewhite
parameters.label: Input Reference White
parameters.description: When converting the input color values to the Working Color Space, this controls how they should be treated with respect to . If the Working Color Space is the same White, then no adjustment is done.

parameters.name: loadingerrorimage
parameters.label: Loading/Error Image
parameters.description: When the file can not be loaded for some reason, select what to display instead.

parameters.name: trim
parameters.label: Trim
parameters.description: Enables the parameters below to set trim in and out points.

parameters.name: tstart
parameters.label: Trim Start
parameters.description: Trim the starting point of the movie.

parameters.name: tstartunit
parameters.label: Trim Start Unit
parameters.description: Select the units for this parameter from Index, Frames, Seconds, and Fraction (percentage).

parameters.name: tend
parameters.label: Trim End
parameters.description: Trim the ending point of the movie.

parameters.name: tendunit
parameters.label: Trim End Unit
parameters.description: Select the units for this parameter from Index, Frames, Seconds, and Fraction (percentage).

parameters.name: textendleft
parameters.label: Extend Left
parameters.description: Determines how the Movie File In handles movie positions that lie before the Trim Start position. For example, if Trim Start is set to 1, and the movie's current index is -10, the Extend Left menu det

parameters.name: textendright
parameters.label: Extend Right
parameters.description: Determines how the Movie File In handles movie positions that lie after the Trim End position. For example, if Trim End is set to 20, and the movie's current index is 25, the Extend Right menu determi

parameters.name: overridesample
parameters.label: Override Sample Rate
parameters.description: Turn On to change the sample rate of the movie. When loading an image sequence, use these parameters to set the playback speed for the sequence.

parameters.name: samplerate
parameters.label: Sample Rate
parameters.description: Set the sample rate for playback when 'Override ' above is On.

parameters.name: prereadframes
parameters.label: Pre-Read Frames
parameters.description: Sets how many video frames TouchDesigner reads ahead and stores in memory. The Movie File In will read and decode frames of the movie into CPU memory before they are used, this can eliminate pops or s

parameters.name: frametimeout
parameters.label: Frame Read Timeout
parameters.description: The time (in milliseconds) TouchDesigner will wait for a frame from the hard drive before giving up. If the Disk Read Timeout time is reached, that frame is simply skipped. This also works for network

parameters.name: frametimeoutstrat
parameters.label: Frame Timeout Strategy
parameters.description: When on, if the Disk Read Timeout is reached TouchDesigner will use the latest available frame in place of the skipped frame.

parameters.name: alwaysloadinitial
parameters.label: Always Load Initial Frame
parameters.description: If this parameter is turned on, then for the first loaded frame the Frame Read Timeout will be ignored, and it will always wait for the first frame to ensure the node always starts up with a valid ima

parameters.name: opentimeout
parameters.label: File Open Timeout
parameters.description: The time (in milliseconds) TouchDesigner will wait for a movie to open. If the Disk Open Timout is reached, the Movie File In will stop waiting and make its image all black, with a grey square in the

parameters.name: predownloadhttp
parameters.label: Pre-Download HTTP Addresses
parameters.description: When On, the file will be fully downloaded before playing. When Off, the file will be streamed from its currently URL.

parameters.name: updateimage
parameters.label: Update Image
parameters.description: Image will not update when set to 0. Movie index will continue to move forward but the output image will not update. This is useful when you are using a Movie Audio to get audio from the movie, but yo

parameters.name: maxdecodecpus
parameters.label: Max Decode CPUs
parameters.description: Limit the maximum number of CPUs that will be used to decode certain video codecs that are capable of multi-CPU decoding, such as H264/H265 and Cineform.	Note that this does not affect multi-threaded

parameters.name: highperfread
parameters.label: High Performance Read
parameters.description: This option should be used when playing back files that require very high SSD read speeds such as high resolution (4K+) HAP Q files. It greatly improves read performance in those cases. It should not

parameters.name: highperfreadfactor
parameters.label: High Performance Read Factor
parameters.description: When doing high performance reads, this parameter controls the size of the read operations that are done on disk. Whatever the largest operation the codec asks to be done, this is multiplied by the re

parameters.name: hwdecode
parameters.label: Hardware Decode
parameters.description: Controls if this node should use hardware decoding via the Nvidia hardware decoder chip. You can check if hardware decoding is being used using the Info , 'hardware_decode' channel. This parameter doe
